title: ACI 318 Flexural Crack Width Limits and Gergely-Lutz Approximation
content: ACI 318 limits maximum flexural crack widths in reinforced concrete: 0.33 mm for exterior exposure (aggressive environment) and 0.41 mm for interior exposure. The Gergely-Lutz formula estimates crack width: w=0.076·β·fs·∛(dc·A) (imperial), where β is the ratio of distances from neutral axis to tension face and steel centroid, fs is steel stress, dc is cover depth, and A is the concrete area per bar. Crack growth rate distinguishes arrested (stable) from propagating (active distress) cracks.
